text: Year of the World 3126, Year before Christ 879.; 4 Kings xi.

Seventh. Vatican Septuagint, by mistake, has "the eighth."<hr>Encouraged, or "strengthened." (Septuagint, etc.) (Haydock)<hr>Captains, whether Levites, or ancient officers of the crown, who were dissatisfied with the usurper, 4 Kings xi.

Israel, of which Juda formed a part. (Menochius)<hr>At the time when the author lived, Israel was destroyed, so that there was no danger of a mistake.<hr>Jerusalem, on a sabbath-day, and most likely on one of the three great festivals: (Calmet) Salien (the year of the world 3157) thinks at the Passover, to avoid suspicion. (Menochius)
Do. Though God's promise was absolute, human means were to be used. (Worthington)

To the sabbath. That is, to perform in your weeks the functions of your office, or the weekly watches. (Challoner)<hr>Porters. Septuagint, "even to the entrance gates."<hr>House, where Athalia resided, or in the apartments of Joas in the temple.<hr>Foundation. Septuagint, "middle." Syriac, "of the cooks, or guards." (Haydock)<hr>It led from the palace up the steps, ver. 21. (Menochius)<hr>Perhaps it was also called Sur, 4 Kings.<hr>But let. This seems to be transposed out of its place from ver. 6.
Slain. Athalia ventured to enter; but she was alone, and not much feared. (Calmet)
High. Hebrew, "the priest dismissed not the courses." (Haydock)<hr>In case of right and necessity, we see here what the high priest could do, though otherwise he would not interfere. (Worthington)
Altar of holocausts, in the court of the priests, while the people stood in their own court. (Tirinus)

Crown. Literally, "diadem."<hr>Testimony. This is explained in the following sentence, (Haydock) which is not in Hebrew nor in many Latin manuscripts. The Jews understand the phylacteries, Deuteronomy 17:18.

Step. Hebrew hamudo, "his pillar," to which he ascended by steps. (Du Hamel)<hr>Entrance into the court of the priests; but the tribune stood in that of the people, where Joas was in his royal attire.
Himself, as God's vicegerent. (Calmet)<hr>A double covenant was made between God and the people, and between the king and his subjects. (Du Hamel)
Idols. Protestants, "images." (Haydock)<hr>Those are wilfully blind who will not distinguish these from the images of Christ and his saints. (Worthington)
David, which had been long neglected, 1 Paralipomenon xxiv. (Menochius)
Upper gate of the guards, at the back or western end of the temple, 2 Paralipomenon 5:5.
